title: M38-series explorer droid

Developed by the almost unknown [[LesTech]] company, the '''M38-series explorer [[Droid/Legends|droid]]''' was created to be an all-purpose assistant to [[Planet/Legends|planetary]] [[Scout/Legends|scouting]] expeditions.

==Characteristics==
The [[Droid/Legends|droid]] was equipped with a plethora of scanning systems, ranging from visual to seismic, and was also fitted with a [[Camera/Legends|camera]] in case they were destroyed. Heavily [[Armor/Legends|armored]], the droid could withstand hazardous environments which many organics could not. The M38 also came equipped with a five-[[Kilogram/Legends|kilogram]] specimen hopper to assist in collecting local [[Plant/Legends|plant]] and animal life. Its two arms ended with three fingered hands; the left one designed for finer manipulation while the right was for sturdier tasks.<ref name="EGD" />

==History==
[[File:M38ExplorerDroid2-FF59.png|thumb|left|The back of an M38 explorer droid]]
While LesTech believed they had created the ultimate [[Scout/Legends|scouting]] aid, [[Planet/Legends|planetary]] scouts thought quite the opposite. Despite the advertising campaign describing the M38 as the ultimate scouting tool, most scouts felt threatened by the droid. A number of scouts refused to work with M38s due to fear of being replaced by the droid.<ref name="SGD">''[[Scavenger's Guide to Droids]]''</ref>

The series also suffered due to its low user-friendliness. The M38 was a very sensitive droid, and occasionally would freeze due to information overload. The droid also suffered many problems with its treadwheel locomotion drive system. The drive system of the M38 was very sensitive to changes in [[Gravity/Legends|gravity]] and terrain, and would require service before and after each mission. If the unit did not receive proper maintenance, the treads would lock up or become unresponsive. Many an M38 was lost over a cliff due to a lack of proper maintenance.

Their sensors, coupled with the units' toughened [[Durasteel/Legends|durasteel]] armor plating, gave them an advantage over [[Arakyd Industries/Legends|Arakyd]] [[Probe droid/Legends|probe droids]], but Arakyd's models had the drop on M38s—the units were slow and short range, and lacked weapons of any kind. Galactic scouts often tried arming the droids with defensive stun blasters, but the M38s did not respond well to non-spec modifications.<ref name="EGD" />

All these problems coupled with a price tag of 13,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] diminished the line in the average scout's eyes.

The [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] used several of these droids to survey planet surfaces and compile lists of viable strategic firing points, which the M38s were surprisingly good at.<ref name="EGD" />

==Behind the scenes==
The picture used in ''[[The Rebel Alliance Sourcebook]]'' to represent the M38 explorer droid was used in the ''[[Death Star Technical Companion]]'' to erroneously illustrate the [[MN-2E general maintenance droid]].
